fields.description:
## Context
Operation certificates become asymmetric on the foundation substrate (namespace `rebar.opcert.v1`),
signed by an environment's Ed25519 key and verifiable by anyone against its public key, binding the
ticket/state so they cannot be replayed. New module `src/rebar/attest/opcert.py`, mirroring
`attest/authorship.py`, registers `rebar.opcert.v1` in the `registry.py` `POLICY` table pinned to
the `sshsig` scheme, and is wired in `attest/__init__.py`.

**Environment-key era rotation (folded in from former task 5893) ŌĆö explicit-SHA model.** Env keys
live **out-of-band** (`.rebar/trusted_environments.yaml`, on the code branch, NOT the tickets
branch ŌĆö see the epic's "Trust config & era-validity protocol"), so the identity epic's
tickets-branch resolver (`authorship.resolve_event_commit` / `_keyring_for`, which glob
`<ticket_dir>/<position>-*.json`) **cannot be reused verbatim**. Instead each key record carries
explicit `added_at_commit`/`revoked_at_commit` **git SHAs on `main`**. What is reused is the
era-validity **rule** ŌĆö the `git merge-base --is-ancestor` ancestry comparison and the shared
`key_not_valid_at_era` verdict ŌĆö factored so authorship (positionŌåÆcommit) and op-cert
(explicit-commit) share one ancestry predicate.

**Two-phase verdict (matches `verify_authorship.py`).** Like authorship, the substrate's era check
returns a non-verified `Verdict` (`unknown_principal`) when no key is valid at the era; the
`key_not_valid_at_era` reclassification is done by the **op-cert verifier** (the analog of the
gate-layer two-phase check): first verify against ANY historical key for the env; if that passes
but the era-scoped check fails, emit `key_not_valid_at_era`; otherwise emit `mismatch` (all
verdicts are canonical `verify_signature_result.schema.json` enum members).

**Material fingerprint = the existing canonical gate fingerprint (opaque to the substrate).** The
`material_fingerprint` bound in the subject is exactly the string the gates already compute today
in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/pass1.py::material_fingerprint`: the 16-hex-char truncation of
`sha256(json.dumps({ticket_id, description, file_impact, children}, sort_keys=True))` (tags /
comments / links / assignee are NOT material). The op-cert substrate (`opcert.py`) treats this
fingerprint as an **opaque string**: `sign_opcert`/`verify_opcert` bind and byte-compare it
exactly, and the caller (the plan-review / completion-verifier gate) supplies it ŌĆö so the substrate
does not depend on `PlanContext`. Replay/mutation defense is therefore: the verifier is given the
ticket's CURRENT fingerprint and rejects any cert whose bound fingerprint differs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Op-certs are produced/verified via the foundation substrate (`src/rebar/attest/opcert.py`,
      `register_opcert_policy()` pinning `rebar.opcert.v1` ŌåÆ `sshsig` in `registry.POLICY`,
      registered in `attest/__init__.py`) with an Ed25519 environment key; a test asserts a cert
      signed by env E verifies against E's public key and fails against a different key.
- [ ] The certificate subject is an in-toto v1 Statement binding
      `{ticket id, material fingerprint, merged-log position (a `main` commit SHA)}`, where the
      material fingerprint is the existing canonical gate fingerprint
      (`plan_review.pass1.material_fingerprint`, 16-hex sha256 over
      `{ticket_id, description, file_impact, children}`) supplied by the caller and treated as an
      opaque bound string by the substrate; a test asserts a cert cannot be replayed onto a
      different ticket or onto a mutated material fingerprint.
- [ ] Environment-key rotation uses the **explicit-SHA** era model: a key is valid at the cert's
      bound `main` commit `P` iff `is-ancestor(added_at_commit, P)` and not
      `is-ancestor(revoked_at_commit, P)` (`git merge-base --is-ancestor`), reusing the shared
      ancestry *rule*, NOT `resolve_event_commit`. The op-cert verifier does the two-phase check
      (verify against any historical key; if that passes but the era check fails ŌåÆ the shared
      `key_not_valid_at_era` verdict; else `mismatch`). All three verdicts (`certified`,
      `key_not_valid_at_era`, `mismatch`) are in the canonical `verify_signature_result.schema.json`
      enum ŌĆö no new verdict string is introduced. A test asserts a cert from a key valid
      at its era verifies while one past its rotation surfaces `key_not_valid_at_era`.
